business.headline: Sensex, Nifty open flat as Middle East tensions keep investors cautiousThe S&P BSE Sensex slipped 22.10 points, or 0.03%, to 75,987.60 in early trade, while the NSE Nifty50 declined 6.95 points, or 0.03%, to 23,906.75.

Byju Raveendran sentenced to six months in jail by Singapore court: ReportByju Raveendran, founder of embattled edtech company Byju's, has been sentenced to six months in jail by a Singapore court for contempt of court.

8th Pay Commission: Odisha consultations scheduled for July 6-7The 8th Pay Commission will visit Bhubaneswar on July 6 and 7 for consultations with organisations, unions and employee associations. The outreach comes as the panel widens regional talks and gathers demands on pay, allowances and pensions.

Indian economy is quite robust: Arvind PanagariyaIn an exclusive interview with India Today, Arvind Panagariya, Chairman of the sixteenth Finance Commission, asserts that the domestic economy remains fundamentally robust, citing a steady gross foreign direct investment of ninety-four billion dollars for the recent fiscal year and consistent growth averaging over seven percent in the past three years.

When centralised manufacturing, record irregularities conveniently escaped detectionIn the eighth part of this series on labour laws and Inspector Raj, this article examines how seemingly separate establishments functioned as one operational unit, while material irregularities in records and manufacturing activity went unnoticed.

Hidden cost of AI adoption: Why enterprises are worried about token economicsEnterprises are reassessing AI roll-outs as token and compute costs climb with wider internal use. The shift is pushing companies to focus on cost control, ROI and sustainable scale.

When it rains, it pours: Why Nithin Kamath thinks 2026 could be tough for householdsIn a post on X, Kamath flagged concerns around a possible super El Nino, weak rainfall and rising energy costs linked to global tensions. According to him, if food and fuel prices rise together, inflation could become a much bigger challenge for Indian households.

ITR filing this year? 5 changes that make it different from last yearPlanning to file your ITR soon? Before you begin, it may be worth slowing down for a moment. From new disclosure requirements to tweaks in reporting rules, tax return filing this year comes with a few important changes that many taxpayers may easily miss. Here are five key updates to know.

Markets end lower as renewed Iran tensions push rupee downThe S&P BSE Sensex fell 479.26 points, or 0.63%, to close at 76,009.70, while the NSE Nifty50 declined 118 points, or 0.49%, to settle at 23,913.70.

Rs 24 lakh for 1 training session from AI gurus? Wall Street says it's worth itFormer SoftBank investors Felipe Sinisterra and Dave Wang are charging banks nearly Rs 24 lakh for one-day AI workshops. The rush for these sessions reflects how AI is becoming essential to productivity, hiring and career survival in finance.

21–30 year olds drive 46% of education loans: Why are young Indians borrowing?Are young Indians borrowing only for emergencies and shopping anymore? Not quite. A growing number are now taking loans for something very different, i.e., learning new skills and improving career prospects. Let's have a look.

What if 8th Pay Commission gets delayed? Here's the impact on salary, HRA and arrearsThe 8th Pay Commission is moving through consultations, but many employees have one key question i.e., what if implementation gets delayed? The answer could have implications for salary, HRA and arrears.
Rupee is falling but is it alarming?The rupee's recent slide has sparked concern, but is it truly a sign of economic stress or just a reflection of global forces at play?

How tiny Taiwan overtook India to become the 5th largest stock marketTaiwan's stock market value has climbed to $4.95 trillion, moving ahead of India's $4.92 trillion market capitalisation. Taiwan now ranks behind only the US, mainland China, Japan and Hong Kong in global equity market rankings.

Oil jumps as US strikes in Iran unsettle markets despite deal talksUS strikes in southern Iran jolted oil markets even as Washington and Tehran pursued a draft war deal in Doha. The moves underscored how quickly the Strait of Hormuz crisis could swing between de-escalation hopes and fresh disruption.

Dalal Street opens steady as investors track US-Iran conflictThe S&P BSE Sensex was up 47.74 points, or 0.06%, at 76,536.70 in early trade, while the NSE Nifty50 gained 31.90 points, or 0.13%, to trade at 24,063.15.

Sensex, Nifty opening: Will stock market continue its rally today?Markets are set for a cautious Tuesday open as investors assess fresh US strikes in southern Iran after Monday's sharp rally. Renewed tension has pushed Brent crude to $98 again, keeping inflation, the rupee and expiry-day volatility in focus.

Economist Montek Singh Ahluwalia says RBI was right to let rupee weakenMontek Singh Ahluwalia said the RBI was right to let the rupee weaken amid external pressures. He said the move reflected market conditions, could aid exports and highlighted wider reform needs.

CNG prices hiked by Rs 2 per kg, cumulative rise of Rs 7 in 11 daysThis is the third CNG price hike in the last nine days and the fourth increase within 11 days. Earlier, CNG prices were raised by Re 1 per kg on Sunday.

Fuel price hike essential, says Montek Singh AhluwaliaIn an exclusive interview with India Today, former Deputy Chairman of the Planning Commission Montek Singh Ahluwalia discussed the fuel price hikes, current economic crisis and the need for structural reforms.

entertainment.headline: The Burj Khalifa dream: What it really costs, celebs who own luxury homes there The Burj Khalifa houses some of the world's most expensive apartments, with prices ranging from a few crores to over Rs 200 crore. Over the years, several Bollywood stars, global celebrities and business elites have reportedly bought homes inside Dubai's most iconic address.

Ravi Kishan files complaint over defamatory claims damaging his reputation Ravi Kishan has filed an FIR alleging false and defamatory statements were spread against him across Gorakhpur. The complaint seeks an impartial investigation and legal action against those involved.

Item songs in 2026? Ram Charan's Peddi and special tracks that don't feel special Ram Charan's Peddi has released Hellallallo with Shruti Haasan and Janhvi Kapoor as a big promotional spectacle. The backlash to the song has renewed questions over repetitive choreography, the male gaze and the fading pull of special numbers.

Drishyam 3 box office Day 6: Mohanlal film beats Aadujeevitham lifetime earnings Drishyam 3 stayed steady on its first Tuesday and crossed Rs 170.32 crore worldwide in six days. The Malayalam version continued to drive the film's strong box office run.

Inside Jennifer Lopez's poolside Memorial Day celebrations with twins and friends Jennifer Lopez gave a glimpse into her poolside Memorial Day moments with family and friends at her California home. The actor shared pictures from the celebrations on Instagram.

Kylie Jenner-Timothee Chalamet courtside PDA goes viral after Knicks' win. Watch Timothee Chalamet and Kylie Jenner celebrated courtside as the New York Knicks secured an NBA Finals spot by defeating the Cleveland Cavaliers. Videos of the couple have now surfaced online.

Zayn Malik says viral Manchester video misunderstood what really happened Singer Zayn Malik addressed the viral Manchester hotel video and denied lashing out at fans, claiming the situation involved men blocking exits for several minutes.

CBS faces heat for taking down Stephen Colbert's Only in Monroe clips CBS sent copyright notices after Stephen Colbert's surprise return to Only in Monroe spread on YouTube. The move drew criticism before the network paused further enforcement pending review.

Sunny Deol waits for wife Pooja as couple makes rare public appearance. Watch Sunny Deol and wife Pooja Deol made a rare appearance together at the Mumbai airport, giving fans an unusual glimpse into the actor's private family life.

Priyanka Chopra visits Mahesh Babu's AMB Cinemas with Namrata, Sitara Priyanka Chopra visited Mahesh Babu's AMB Cinemas in Hyderabad with Namrata Shirodkar and Sitara Ghattamaneni. Photos from the outing sparked fan reactions as her Varanasi shoot continued in the city.

From AIB Knockout to FWICE ban: Ranveer Singh's most controversial moments Actor Ranveer Singh's latest Don 3 controversy has revived discussion around the actor's most talked-about public rows, from AIB Knockout to his nude photoshoot.
Ashutosh Rana and Renuka Shahane renew vows on 25th wedding anniversary. Video Ashutosh Rana and Renuka Shahane renewed their wedding vows by exchanging garlands on their 25th anniversary.

FIR against Dhurandhar production designer over sexual harassment allegations Chandigarh Police have registered a case against Saini S Johray, the production designer of Dhurandhar, following a complaint by a woman accusing him of sexual harassment, wrongful confinement and spiking her drink.

Non-cooperation, not ban: Ashoke Pandit on action against Ranveer Singh in Don 3 row Ashoke Pandit said FWICE (The Federation of Western India Cine Employees) has issued a non-cooperation directive against Ranveer Singh after his Don 3 exit. He said the federation cannot ban anyone and acted because abrupt exits can hurt producers financially.

You hurt workers, not stars: Sanjay Gupta criticises FWICE ban on Ranveer Singh Filmmaker Sanjay Gupta has questioned FWICE's decision to ban Ranveer Singh after his exit from Don 3. The dispute has sharpened after Farhan Akhtar alleged Rs 45 crore in losses and the federation issued a non-cooperation directive.

Mouni Roy shares post for 'little sister' Disha Patani, deletes it later Mouni Roy called Disha Patani her "little sister" in a post that she later deleted. The timing drew attention because Disha had recently faced trolling over rumours surrounding Mouni's split from Suraj Nambiar.

Mango Pachcha trailer: Kichcha Sudeep's nephew Sanchith debuts in gritty crime drama The trailer of Kichcha Sudeep's nephew, Sanchith Sanjeev's debut film Mango Pachcha, is out and presents a world filled with crime, narcotics, and gore, promising a thrilling ride through Mysuru's darker side in the early 2000s.

Actor Vishal calls Vijay most prolific CM for allowing five theatre shows a day Actor Vishal thanked Tamil Nadu Chief Minister C Joseph Vijay after the state allowed theatres to screen five shows a day for new Tamil films in their first week.

Zoya Akhtar's Tiger Baby Films hit by major data theft, office boy arrested Mumbai Police have arrested two men for allegedly stealing hard disks from Tiger Baby's Bandra office. The missing drives carried unreleased film and web series material, with losses estimated at over Rs 13 lakh.

general news.headline: ED heat on ex-Kerala CM Pinarayi Vijayan in corruption case linked to daughterThe raids were part of the ED's ongoing probe into alleged financial irregularities involving Cochin Minerals and Rutile Limited and Exalogic Solutions, a private company owned by Pinarayi Vijayan's daughter T Veena.

Byju Raveendran sentenced to six months in jail by Singapore court: ReportByju Raveendran, founder of embattled edtech company Byju's, has been sentenced to six months in jail by a Singapore court for contempt of court.

OBC face, bigger 2029 role: What Congress offered Siddaramaiah to step down as CMA marathon seven-hour round of meetings, a high-stakes Delhi offer, and an uncertain Chief Minister at the centre of it all - Congress's Karnataka reset plan may have triggered its biggest internal power struggle since 2023.

CBSE denies breach claims as hacker sparks 'battle of URLs'CBSE has denied claims made by a 19-year-old over alleged vulnerabilities in its evaluation system, saying the cited portal was only a testing site with sample data and was not linked to the original platform used for assessment work.

Who is afraid of the Cockroach and whyThe dramatic rise of the so-called Cockroach Janta Party (CJP) reflects a growing anger at a political system so broken that protestors have turned to the imagery of an insect that thrives in gutters and drains to make a larger point about the darkness threatening governance and democracy.

Live: Siddaramaiah's son Yathindra likely to get Cabinet berthKarnataka Leadership Buzz Live Updates: Karnataka's political landscape may be headed for a dramatic reset, with Congress sources claiming Chief Minister Siddaramaiah is now likely to tender his resignation on Thursday following marathon discussions with the party high command in Delhi. Sources said Siddaramaiah is also expected to address a press conference in Bengaluru on Wednesday, a development being closely tracked amid intensifying speculation over a leadership transition in the Congress-ruled state. Deputy CM DK Shivakumar has extended his stay in Delhi and is scheduled to attend a breakfast meeting called by Siddaramaiah at the CM's residence in Bengaluru tomorrow morning. The high-voltage political developments unfolded after nearly seven hours of meetings involving Congress president Mallikarjun Kharge, senior leader Rahul Gandhi and top Karnataka leaders, even as the party publicly maintained that discussions were limited to Rajya Sabha and Legislative Council polls. Stay tuned to indiatoday.in for all the latest updates.

Beyond Delhi Gymkhana, the 170-acre elite fortress called Delhi Golf ClubThe eviction notice to the Delhi Gymkhana, located on a 27-acre plot, has sparked a conversation around the Delhi Golf Club, which is based on 170 acres of prime government land and has a restrictive membership model. The golf club has faced questions over racism, public access, golfers' practice rights and an ASI-protected monument.

Pakistani extremists prevent Lahore from returning to original Hindu, Sikh namesWeeks after clearing the proposal, the Maryam Nawaz-led provincial government of Pakistan's Punjab has deferred the decision to restore the pre-Partition Hindu and British-era names of several Lahore landmarks. According to reports, the government was forced to make a U-turn after it faced criticism from "extremists" and social media vloggers.

The Patidar Way: RCB captain, under the radar and above the restRCB vs GT, IPL 2026 Qualifier 1: In a league built on personality and spectacle, Rajat Patidar is a quiet anomaly. He says little, seeks nothing, and delivers everything. RCB's captain is unlike anyone the IPL has seen before.

Beaten, starved, raped: Wave of child abuse scandal rocks over 100 French schoolsProsecutors say investigations are now under way involving 84 preschools, around 20 primary schools and nearly 10 daycare centres across the capital.

India has more cities than it knows of. Why are they hiding in plain sight?India might have far more cities and towns than the government's numbers acknowledge. An EAC-PM paper argues that millions live in human settlements that function like towns and cities but officially remain classified as rural. This adversely impacts the lives of millions of residents.

Microsoft and others rethink AI use, cost rising fast and no productivity gainsHandcrafted ByOur Editors AI was supposed to save money. But as AI meets messy realities of work, tech companies are curbing their enthusiasm. Microsoft is now going easy on Claude due to its cost, while Uber has in just 5 months exhausted its entire AI budget for 2026.

Record 4 lakh+ CBSE students requested answer sheets as OSM faces questionsCBSE has released fresh figures on Class 12 scanned answer book requests and said pending copies will be shared by May 27. The update comes amid questions over alleged discrepancies in scanned answer sheets and the board's On-Screen Marking system.

Sponsoring terrorism will have consequences: India tears into Pak at UNSpeaking at the UNSC Open Debate in New York, India's Permanent Representative to the UN, Ambassador Harish Parvathaneni, accused Pakistan of decades-long support for terrorism, religious extremism and violent radicalism.
The CIA, the T-72, and the Delhi Gymkhana Club - a Cold War taleHow the Delhi Gymkhana Club was the stage for a CIA intelligence coup involving the Indian Army's Soviet-built tank fleet.

'Mohammad Deepak', who stood up for Muslim shopkeeper, may lose gym over rent duesA resident of Kotdwar, 'Mohammad Deepak' said his business suffered heavily after the viral incident. Earlier, around 150 members regularly visited 'Hulk Gym', but the number dropped sharply after the controversy and the tense atmosphere that followed.

Wives not deemed maids, refusal to do household chores not cruelty: High CourtThe Bombay High Court set aside a divorce granted to a husband who alleged cruelty over household chores. It said marriage is a partnership of equals and ordered Rs 20,000 monthly maintenance for the wife.

Delhi woman calls technicians to fix AC, what they do next leaves her emotionalDuring an AC service visit in Delhi, two technicians quietly paused to admire a woman's artwork at home. The simple exchange gave viewers as a reminder of how creativity can create unexpected human connections.
A home on the Moon: Nasa announces roadmap to build Moon baseAfter returning astronauts to deep space, Nasa is now laying the groundwork for something humanity has never attempted before.

Who is Suraj Pasi, the Gen-Z leader at centre of Lucknow's temple-mosque dispute?A long-running dispute over Lucknow's Kasmandi Kala fort has reignited ahead of Bakri Eid, with the Pasi and Muslim communities asserting rival claims over the site. The row has put Lakhan Army leader Suraj Pasi at the centre of protests as police step up security.

Video: Illegal migrants flock border to return as Bengal starts deportation driveSeveral suspected illegal Bangladeshi migrants reached border checkpoints after deportation announcements. The movement follows Suvendu Adhikari's declaration of a "detect, delete and deport" policy targeting infiltrators.

Kerala misses early date with monsoon: When will it rain?India's most awaited season was expected early this year, but shifting skies and weather conditions have changed the timeline. Here's when monsoon rains are now expected to arrive.

sports.headline: India Women's pre-T20 World Cup checklist: Key areas to fix in England series Women's T20 World Cup 2026: Harmanpreet Kaur's India have plenty to focus on in the series against England as they build towards their quest for a maiden world T20 title.

We are coming for you: Patidar sounds warning after RCB storm into IPL final RCB vs GT, IPL 2026 Qualifier 1: Rajat Patidar played a sensational innings to take RCB into the final of IPL 2026. After hammering Gujarat Titans, Patidar sounded a warning to the bowlers – we are coming for you.

The Patidar Way: RCB captain, under the radar and above the rest RCB vs GT, IPL 2026 Qualifier 1: In a league built on personality and spectacle, Rajat Patidar is a quiet anomaly. He says little, seeks nothing, and delivers everything. RCB's captain is unlike anyone the IPL has seen before.

Qualifier 1 Play of the Day: When Shubman Gill's gamble backfired on Gujarat IPL Qualifier 1, Play of the Day: Gujarat Titans' batting line-up tanked in Dharamsala on Tuesday, as they tried chasing a record total in the IPL playoffs. But batting failure was not why the team lost, nor was their bowling performance in the last 5 overs, where they gave away 104 runs.

Virat Kohli, de Villiers share nostalgic hug after RCB charge into finals of IPL RCB thrashed Gujarat Titans by 92 runs to reach a second straight IPL final, with Virat Kohli embracing AB de Villiers after the finish. The result kept their title defence alive and turned an old partnership into the night's enduring image.

Zee Entertainment in talks with FIFA over World Cup broadcast rights in India India's Zee Entertainment Enterprises is in talks with FIFA to secure the streaming and broadcast rights for the 2026 FIFA World Cup in India, the company said on Tuesday, a move that could shake up the country's sports broadcasting landscape.

I feel like the Eiffel Tower: Naomi Osaka revels in dramatic French Open fashion Japan's former world number one Naomi Osaka said she is beginning to embrace her role as an entertainer on the WTA Tour after turning heads with a dazzling golden outfit during her first-round win at the French Open on Tuesday.

Ee Salavu Cup Namde? Ruthless RCB demolish GT to book direct ticket to final RCB vs GT, IPL Qualifier 1: Inevitable? Rajat Patidar's 33-ball 93 anchored a record 254, before RCB's bowlers decimated Gujarat Titans with ease. In Dharamsala on Tuesday, Royal Challengers Bengaluru booked their place in a second consecutive IPL final, one win away from history.

Sriram Balaji, Yuki Bhambri make winning start to French Open in men's doubles India's Yuki Bhambri and N Sriram Balaji moved into the second round of the men's doubles event at the French Open 2026 after winning their respective opening-round matches in Paris on Tuesday.

French teenager becomes youngest male Grand Slam match-winner in 17 years Wildcard teenager Moise Kouame stunned former champion Marin Cilic on his Grand Slam debut at Roland Garros. The straight-sets win underlined a rapid rise and delivered a rare age-record breakthrough.

A 3-hour ad film? Viral video takes cheeky dig at IPL's commercial overdrive IPL 2026: Popular X personality Ramesh Srivats takes a cheeky swipe at the relentless advertising creeping into modern cricket broadcasts, as multi-billion dollar team sales and massive media rights push IPL's commercial engine into a dizzying overdrive.

Bizarre: Sai Sudharsan throws his bat onto the stumps, out hit-wicket in Qualifier 1Gujarat Titans opener Sai Sudharsan suffered one of the most bizarre dismissals in IPL history during the Qualifier 1 clash against Royal Challengers Bengaluru. Attempting to play a cut shot, Sudharsan accidentally flung his bat onto the stumps behind him, resulting in a freak hit-wicket dismissal.

Scorching Paris heat? Coco Gauff keeps it quick for fans at Roland GarrosFrench Open 2026: Coco Gauff beat Taylor Townsend 6-4, 6-0 to begin her French Open title defence in punishing Paris heat. After a nervy start, the defending champion found her calm, handled the conditions and moved into the second round.

England add Danni Wyatt-Hodge, Maia Bouchier for women's T20Is against IndiaEngland opener Danni Wyatt-Hodge will be available for the second and third T20Is against India following the birth of her first child. England and India will face off in the series beginning at Chelmsford on Thursday ahead of the women's T20 World Cup next month.

Qualifier 1: Virat Kohli's 10-year-wait for fifty in IPL playoffs continuesVirat Kohli's has not scored a fifty in the IPL playoffs since 2026. Kohli has played 17 IPL innings in his life, where he has scored 396 runs with a strike-rate of 121.

Vaibhav Sooryavanshi's maturity amazes Shanaka: Haven't seen a kid like thatVaibhav Sooryavanshi has taken the IPL by storm with his fearless batting, but it is his calmness and maturity off the field that has left Rajasthan Royals teammate Dasun Shanaka most impressed.

Afghanistan announce squads for India tour, Rashid Khan skips one-off TestAfghanistan announced their squads for the upcoming tour of India on Tuesday, confirming that star spinner Rashid Khan will skip the one-off Test match but return for the ODI series beginning later in June.

Aryna Sabalenka responds to Djokovic, shows off dance moves after French Open winFrench Open: Aryna Sabalenka answered Novak Djokovic's viral battle dance after winning her French Open opener. Their playful exchange gave Roland Garros an early talking point and exposed a lighter edge to elite tennis.

Days after domestic retirement, Vijay Shankar joins Kandy Royals in LPLFormer India all-rounder Vijay Shankar has quickly moved onto the overseas franchise circuit after announcing his retirement from Indian domestic cricket and the IPL, signing with the Kandy Royals for the 2026 season of the Lanka Premier League. The 35-year-old headlines the tournament's marquee signings ahead of the player draft scheduled for June 1.

RCB vs GT: No Phil Salt. GT make shock addition to team in Qualifier 1IPL 2026: Gujarat Titans made a shocking change to their line-up in Qualifier 1 against RCB. They dropped left-arm seamer Arshad Khan from their line-up.

Ex-Utd player Scott McTominay features on 20 pound bank notes. Are there others?FIFA World Cup: Scott McTominay has been honoured by the Bank of Scotland with a special 20 pound note featuring his iconic overhead kick against Denmark. The Scotland midfielder called the tribute "incredibly special," with the initiative also supporting Crisis while celebrating a defining moment in Scottish football history.

Daniil Medvedev's French Open misery continues with another first-round exitDaniil Medvedev once again fell victim to the French Open's first-round trapdoor, losing 6-2, 1-6, 6-1, 1-6, 6-4 to Australian wildcard Adam Walton on Tuesday in a wildly fluctuating contest on the Paris clay.

Thailand Open: PV Sindhu and Satwik-Chirag storm into quarter-finalsIndia enjoyed a strong day at the Thailand Open as P. V. Sindhu and the men's doubles pair of Satwiksairaj Rankireddy and Chirag Shetty advanced to the quarter-finals with commanding performances on Thursday.

Norway Chess: Carlsen suffers shock defeat, Gukesh, Praggnanandhaa make winning startMagnus Carlsen suffered a rare opening-round defeat to Alireza Firouzja at Norway Chess after a costly time-trouble blunder, with Firouzja converting clinically to take full points. Meanwhile, D Gukesh and R Praggnanandhaa started strongly, both securing Armageddon wins to begin their campaigns on a high.

How Melbourne Stars tried to sign Sachin Tendulkar for a one-off Big Bash gameSachin Tendulkar in the Big Bash with Shane Warne? Melbourne Stars once dreamed of pulling off the wildest signing in Australian cricket history, a one-night spectacle that could have packed the park.

RCB vs GT Highlight: RCB crush GT to reach second-straight finalRCB vs GT Highlight, IPL 2026 Qualifier 1: Royal Challengers Bengaluru stormed into their second successive Indian Premier League final with a crushing 92-run win over Gujarat Titans in Qualifier 1 in Dharamsala on Tuesday. Riding on skipper Rajat Patidar's blistering unbeaten 93, RCB piled up a record 254 for 5 before their pacers dismantled the Titans batting line-up to bowl them out for 162.

South Africa hungrier for T20 World Cup win after double heartbreak: Laura WolvaardtLaura Wolvaardt said South Africa are more motivated than ever to win the Women's T20 World Cup after three successive ICC final defeats, adding that recent heartbreaks have strengthened the team's hunger and resilience.

Day 3 Highlights: Sinner cruises into 2nd round, Jessica Pegula crashes outFrench Open Day 3 Highlights: Jannik Sinner cruised into the second round, but Jessica Pegula suffered a shock early exit on a dramatic day. Daniil Medvedev and Alexander Bublik also crashed out, while Aryna Sabalenka, Coco Gauff, N Sriram and Yuki Bhambri progressed.

5 Red Lights: What we learned from Formula 1's wild Canadian GPThe 2026 Formula 1 season finally came alive in Montreal. From Mercedes' rising teenage star and McLaren's strategy blunders to a vintage Hamilton-Verstappen duel, the Canadian Grand Prix delivered drama, intrigue and a title fight with real bite.

Virat Kohli key, but RCB must avoid one-man dependence in playoffs: Sanjay ManjrekarSanjay Manjrekar has warned Royal Challengers Bengaluru against becoming overly dependent on Virat Kohli during the IPL 2026 playoffs, saying it could push the team into dangerous territory.

Zheng Qinwen breaks down in press conference after shock exit: VideoZheng Qinwen broke down in tears after suffering a shock first-round defeat to Maja Chwalinska at the 2026 French Open, admitting nerves and pressure prevented her from playing her best tennis.

education.headline: The role of data analytics in modern advertising educationThis transformation also exercises a significant impact on the realm of 'advertising education.'

NCERT deletes Azad Pak, adds China's aggression reference in Class 12 textbookThe NCERT has made final changes to its Class 12 Political Science textbook syllabus for the upcoming academic year, including a revision in reference to India's border situation with China, Azad Pakistan, Article 370 and more.

NEET row: Bihar cops find 6 post-dated cheques 'issued for question paper brokers'Six post-dated cheques which were suspected to have been issued in favour of mafia demanding over Rs 30 lakh from each candidate seeking alleged leaked question paper ahead of NEET held last month were recovered by Bihar Police.

Forest fires surge tenfold from 2004 to 2017: Causes, impact and preventionAmidst a relentless surge, intense forest fires are now multiplying tenfold, casting a menacing shadow over our flora and fauna. Year by year, their fiery grip tightens, threatening ecosystems far and wide, now extending their reach to impact human lives.

Fresh plea in Supreme Court to recall NEET results, reconduct examsA fresh petition has been filed in the Supreme Court to recall the National Entrance cum Eligibility Test - Under Graduate (NEET-UG) 2024 results and re-conduct fresh examination after discrepancies in the declared results.

NTA issues clarification after NEET result controversy over toppers, high cut-offIn a detailed statement, the National Testing Agency (NTA) said that the increase in cut-off and number of toppers in the NEET-UG reflect the competitive nature of the exam and maintained that the test's integrity has not been compromised.

Exclusive: Arvind Kejriwal claims 'BJP succession war, PM backing Amit Shah'Speaking exclusively to India Today TV, Delhi Chief Minister Arvind Kejriwal claimed that there is a "succession war" within the BJP. He also claimed that the ruling party is "not fighting the Lok Sabha elections as a team".

4 medical students arrested in Bihar for appearing as proxies for NEET aspirantsFour MBBS students who appeared in place of NEET aspirants in the medical entrance exam were detained in Bihar's Purnea.

NEET-UG question paper not leaked, claims baseless: National Testing AgencyThe reports claiming question paper leak in the medical entrance exam NEET-UG are "completely baseless and without any ground", the National Testing Agency (NTA) has clarified.
The generation with answers: Tech-savvy Indian youth tackling global challengesIndia's tech-savvy youth rewriting the global narrative, one solution at a time.

Left sweeps JNU students' polls, Dhananjay is first Dalit presidentThe Left swept the Jawaharlal Nehru University Students' Union (JNUSU) polls, winning all four posts and trouncing the RSS-affiliated ABVP. The ABVP was leading at one point, but the Left eventually surged ahead and emerged victorious.
The power of knowledge: Education's influence on real estate market trendsIn a dynamic sector like real estate, investing in continuing education is not simply an option; it is a requirement.

JNU students' body election on March 22 after 4 yearsThe Jawaharlal Nehru University Students' Union (JNUSU) polls will take place on March 22, more than four years after the last elections were held in September 2019.

UP 12th board exam math, biology papers leaked on WhatsApp, complaint filedThe mathematics and biology question papers for the Uttar Pradesh class 12 board exam were allegedly leaked on a WhatsApp group.

UP Police Constable recruitment exam cancelled days after paper leakThe Uttar Pradesh Police Constable recruitment exam held on February 17 and 18 was canceled due to a paper leak. The Chief Minister has announced a re-examination within the next six months.
